dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear the gift of Aphrodite, don't you, there? You know, she's much, much older than she looks. Appearances are everything to her, whilst as for me, I'm about hotness of a different sort...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, the flamboyant god of light has put a little sparkle on you, hasn't he? Shines like the sun, Apollo does, oh sure! But there's more than one way to light up a place, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You know Ares by now, don't ya, dearie? He's cool-headed more than some of us, sure, but I think something's wrong with him. The only kind of warmth he cares for comes from fresh-spilled blood and flames of battle and the like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So it was Artemis who sniffed you out, was it? Seems like an awful big coincidence, but then we're gods, these things they happen all the time! I'll say I'm glad she found you when she did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You've quite a lot of Ashes, haven't ya? The remains of the past. Humbling to even think about, that the greatest monuments and forests and whatnot can be reduced to that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not too worried here right now what with Athena holding down the fort. She can be a little much if you two ever meet, but out of everybody here, she'd be the first I'd pick to watch my back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sensing something different in you there, dearie. It's like a divine mark of ours but it's most certainly not anybody's here. It's positively ancient, even next to me! Got some old friends that we don't know about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You should have seen it, dearie, when we brought old Chronos down! Took six of us, at least, but we got him! Little wonder he's all sore about it still. We'll just have to dispose of him more thoroughly! Cremation, mayhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I still remember how it felt when we showed Chronos what we younger generation were made of! Now you're the younger generation, dearie, dealing with the same old problems and worse. Some things never do change!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You and old Hecate, you must be sorting out some way to keep that Chronos from recovering, lest you wouldn't keep on going after him like this! You getting any warmer yet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We've been holding our own up here against that Chronos and his forces, but there's something going on, oh I can feel it. Whatever the Titan thinks he has in store, however, I suspect that it can burn...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We don't quite know what Chronos has been cooking up for us, but we're all thinking that his little recent skirmishes against our mountainside are not the brunt of it. Not keen here to find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Got yourself some Cinder there, don't ya? Not a flashy substance, but it's strong, good source of flame! Most gods and mortals only care for shiny baubles and the like. But some of you witches rather know your stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You're loaded up with Cinder, aren't ya? That much of it I can detect from here... a special kind from torches I know well. Some remnants of your bouts with Hecate? You can't really be fighting. Probably just your nightly exercise!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you've all this Chronos business to sort out but want to say, I can appreciate that you're attempting to make life a little cozier back home. Make things warmer and old Hestia can tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Never been to where you live but I can tell when someone's made a warm and happy home, such as they can. Decorations, little touches for a sense of welcome, that's the stuff! Not everybody gets it but you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You're used to living in darkness, aren't you? Up here, darkness is widely feared. Even in the warmth of my firelight, they jump at shadows, mortals and even gods sometimes! They trust their weak eyes overmuch I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: My frosty little sister Demeter has been in touch already, I can tell! Your nan fancies herself the eldest of Olympus, she does! Only true because I'm usually not here. Mayhaps she left you cold, well not to worry, I have just the remedy!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Poor little sister Demeter. She bore but one child, a girl, dearie. Persephone, your mum! And even still, that child caused her at least as much grief as joy. It's not your mum's fault, mind. She was a lovely one, with hair like yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't even want to know what's going on with Dionysus anymore. The worse things get, the more he's wanting to cut loose! Whole blasted world could be aflame and he'd just let it burn! Could use some of his wine right now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What happened to those Flames of yours? Now shining almost like the break of Dawn. Mayhaps that rosy-fingered Eos has decided to pitch in! Much as we'd hate to interrupt her from the one thing on her schedule each day...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The city of Ephyra burns, but it's not entirely my fault! Flames are like a tool, I suppose... one of those things requiring precaution on one's part. Lose something in a fire, sorry! Can't help you with that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You had a bad run-in with that wretched Eris on your way here, eh? Just great, to know that Strife herself is up against us now! And got her grubby little fingers on my Rail of Adamant... I ought have kept the thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you plucked that nasty Eris from the sky! Well, serves her right! Though we could never make her stay away for good. To think she's one of Nyx's many pups! Squeeze out a big litter like that, you're liable to get a runt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: That Eris keeps on getting in your way, even though Chronos himself has stood down and Typhon's dead and such? Well, she's as stubborn as we are, I am practically impressed!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Things have not exactly gone our way, dearie. 'Specially if what I'm hearing about the Fates is so. Whether we ought to be in charge or no, it's they that put us here... and now they've vanished like a puff of smoke?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You made it all the way down to the Mourning Fields before? A horrid place, or so I hear, and covered in dry brush, far as the eye can see. Well next time you're there, you burn your way on through! Hear me, hot stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You might be wondering, Great-Auntie Hestia, when does the fighting ever cease? Well, I invite you to imagine back when I was closer to your age... a little leaner, little feistier, but not too different, all in all. We've been fighting ever since.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: For every candle that you light, for every cauldron that you boil, that's my flame working its own magick for you, dearie! So you just think of me whenever the sparks fly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Funny thing about flame, dearie, it's warm and pretty, isn't it? But it can kill you in a couple of licks. Really makes you think! And really makes you burn to a crisp, if you're not careful...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Your dad and I got on all right, comparatively speaking, I suppose. After we put the Titans in their place, though, he went down into your Underworld, and I drifted away, like a spark from a flame...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Your dad and I, we've got a lot in common, I suppose. The two not-technically-Olympians! At least I get to visit when I want, whereas poor Hades, soon as he got saddled with the Underworld, they never counted him among their kind again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I sure am glad to know your dad's all right. Him and your mum, your brother, everybody from that House of his. May they all be warm and comfortable together there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The hearth-flame of this mountain started sputtering a bit after Typhon decided to show up. It's since been burning bright and hot, but I've been tending to it plenty, just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Old Hecate's your teacher, is that it? Known her since I was probably your age, right proper dame! Been far too long since last we saw each other, come to think. Eh, what can you do? She hates everybody, I hate everybody.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to know you and Hephaestus get along. That one, he's often on his own, as though my flames are all the company he needs. He's not the worst of sorts as we gods go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Already had a brush with sister Hera, have you, now? She's more accustomed to attending youngsters such as you, but then again, she's got her own to deal with. So why not stick with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Our young Queen Hera, she'd always say one ought to keep her feelings bottled up and carefully in check. Look where that attitude got her! I say let loose your temper and your rage. Let it all out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh while you're up here do watch out for Heracles, big burly sort of man? One of the best we've got but sometimes gives the sense that he's become unhappy with his job. How that is possible, though, is beyond my ken!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Done had a chat with Hera, with Queen Hera I should say, about how maybe we can all go easier on Heracles after all this. He ought to put down that big club of his and have a good long sit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You've got a real spring in your step if I'm not mistaken, dearie! The handiwork of swiftest Hermes, I suppose. You know what else moves awful quick? Here, let me give you a little hint!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: See they've got statues of me up here now, dearie? They didn't use to, but the others, they've been trying to get me back into the fold, pay me respects, there's a nice thought! But I don't want to see my own mug all the time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: How come you're not living in the House of Hades with your mum and dad? Must be nice and cozy there all deep beneath the Earth. Well, a House isn't always a home. Mayhaps you like your woods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Shade of that lad Icarus has been flying about, has he? Seems rather handy having him around, all soaring like a bird. And his new wings don't look like they can melt!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You got my Everlasting Ember, haven't you? Can faintly see its flicker even here. Your way of signaling to your Great-Auntie Hestia, and all the rest, that you are hankering for Boons with just a little bit of spice!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: See how that Everlasting Ember keeps on giving? It's right there in the name, dearie! Glad you saw that it was good for something, means I get to stay plenty active at my age!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Odd sort of flame you've got this eve, if it can even be considered flame at all. So that's one of Nyx's weapons, eh? Changing their shape like the old Infernal Arms would do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We ought have been kinder to mortals, really. Then Chronos would've had a harder time compelling the dead to rise against us! I've always tried to look after the living. But our family's best known for looking after ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortals used to make us many offerings, giving up their own food and the like. They always praised me first among the gods, for it's my fire they used when making sacrifice. It was very sweet!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The others don't agree with me on this, dearie, but you're still young, mayhaps impressionable, so... well I think mortals ought to get from us a bit more than we give. If the Fates are saying it'll happen down the line, then good!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The others, each in their own way, they do like when mortals make them subjects in their tales and songs. Well, not I! In that regard I think you Underworld gods are in the right. Best keep our business to our own damn selves!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Where in those bloody depths are you, dearie, underneath the sea?! It's hard to get a blaze a-roaring when there's water all about, but if you can handle the pressure down there, I can bring the heat!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what this family's problem is, dearie? It's just too big! The others act as though they pity me, poor childless Hestia, they say! But they all long for their childless days, I can see it in their eyes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I almost pity you, dearie, all swept up in our affairs! You don't know how hard I've tried to avoid it myself. Must we always have to take a blasted side in everything? But we gods, we just love a good fight...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I figured we could use you, dearie, no real surprise. But, I didn't expect we'd like you, which is to say you're young, you've got your different customs and whatnot. We can be hard to get along with! But you're doing it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Just earlier I felt the strangest urge to seek you out, dearie! Almost as though it wasn't even me deciding it! Messing with eldritch forces or somesuch? Well if you want to gaze into the cold abyss, I'll keep you nice and warm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know how you got so far so quick tonight, hot stuff, but you're not going any farther till you're properly prepared. Got to bring plenty of heat where you're going!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Still don't quite know how you're getting all that way in but a flash once in a while. Mayhaps some of your witch-magick! Long as you don't sputter out before you get to the end.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You didn't even get the Palace tour, Hera says. Aw, dearie, if only I could have been there to show you around instead of tending to our flames. But then again, you see one of our fancy columns, mayhaps you've seen 'em all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Been on your own all this time, haven't you? Well, might be you're better off. Wish I never knew my blasted dad and mum! They taught me nothing I couldn't have learned better on my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Someday I'd like to see the River Phlegethon you've got down in the Underworld! A river full of flame... tried making something like that here on our side, but it sent mortals panicking, and the gods didn't much care for it either...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You had it out with Polyphemus, so I hear! Recall distinctly how he lost his eye. Let's say flame played a vital part and leave it there. Almost felt sorry for the lad, but he's been getting by. And getting in your way...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Blessed already by the mighty god of earth and sea, are ya? Poseidon's power and my own don't often mix, but then again, neither do he and I, of that you can be certain, there, dearie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So the Titan of Foresight slipped his chains and fights against us now! A pity, that. Been on the outs with us ever since we caught him red-handed with my flames. Stole fire for the sake of mortalkind! Could have just asked me nice!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus, he never seemed to lack for smarts as I recall. Was a right level-headed sort, but now? He's serving Chronos, making mayhem for us here! They say he sees the future, so he must know he won't get away with this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I figured once we got to Chronos, that Prometheus would not be far behind, longing for the days his liver was an eagle's snack. I know he's been a problem, but... I don't want to see him go through that again. Don't properly know why...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I know Prometheus supposedly can see what's coming up, but I just can't believe he saw all this! Suppose I should be madder at him, but... least it seems like he's been looking out for mortals still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Got sent home by Prometheus, I heard. Well if he burned you don't go putting ice on it, no matter what Demeter says. You witches must have ointments and the like...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let us keep on jerking you around, all right? First we got you going after Chronos, now we got you coming here to our defense, blast! Though just in case nobody's said it yet, we really do appreciate the help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, I'm proper tired now, you know? Incinerating this and that, on and on, it's taxing after a while! I'll keep on backing you, dearie, but might not be as much for chitchat for a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You've got Selene's moonlight shine on you, eh? I'll not deny it has a certain power and allure... but such a cold and distant light, next to a scorching flame? There's no comparison!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Something about this night gives me the proper chills, hot stuff. It's like the darkness is so thick, it suffocates! And problem is, you can't have fire without air. Careful, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: It's downright sinister out there; can't hardly see a thing. But you know what? Where there's flame, there's light. So stick with me! Together we can make it through the darkest times.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, this mountaintop. Gave up my seat here, you know. Wanted to see the world, not my blasted sisters everyday! But now I'm stuck here with the lot of them. Burns me right up, even to think about!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess what, hot stuff, your Great-Aunt's back again to turn your foes to soot! Best way to make it so they never bother you again, though tidying up can be a bit of a pain...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Strange contraption that you're using to make everything blow up! I got odd glances too when I still had the Rail of Adamant, but it could make 'em look away right quick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well look at those fancy flames you've got, sputtering their sparks all uncontrollably! That is a safety hazard, dearie. Back when I was shooting fire at my foes, it sure was not an automatic thing. You youngsters have it easy now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, look at you, hot stuff, you made it to the surface after all! I figured that you might. It's maybe even colder here than down below, but that, at least, we can fix right away, I think!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Mortals go from worshiping the flame to fearing it. As well they ought! The hearth-fires of every home there in that wretched city you reside in, all of them burned out. Not that I'm mad, oh no.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Poor thing, been all cooped up inside that Underworld, haven't you? There's a whole great world to see out there, once your business is done. Do let me know, I can suggest some wondrous spots not far from here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not supposed to be up here, are ya? Don't think your dad's permitted back up top, at least. But you're a witch, must be you've got your cures for everything under the sun!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Titans, gods, still couldn't tell you what's the bloody difference anyway! Don't look much different, don't sound much different. Not an age thing, that I can attest! It's just a handy way to brand our enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What's that you got there, Ygnium is it? Reminds me of those twin torches that old Hecate would always have with her. If these are anything like those, they'll never sputter out. But just in case, we'll keep them nice and hot!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Since Typhon's back, I thought I'd say nice knowing you, dearie! Not that we're going down without a fight, but just in case. We barely beat him when we first settled up with Chronos way back when! But now he's grown, a lot!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't quite know how we're managing to hold back Typhon from reducing this whole mountaintop to ash. Must be the lightning, flames, floods, and earthquakes and the like! If you've got any bright ideas yourself, just come on up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: That bloody monster's down and out? And I mean out for good, as in, we can't find any trace of him at all! They're telling me you turned Typhon to ash, dearie! That's proper scary stuff...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Great stuff, dearie, giving bad Chronos and then Typhon the old one-two! You make this stuff look proper easy now. Mayhaps I should give it a go myself one of these nights?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well you are seriously on a hot streak there, hot stuff! No wonder I've been calling you that, eh? Every night you're coming back with something in the bag, you keep that up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So you're a bit of a witch, are you? Well, not I! Stuck with this family already, don't see why I'd want another one. But I understand the appeal, I suppose. Plenty of candles and fire to it, isn't there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, the mighty ruler of Olympus gave to you a bit of his thunderous might, did he now? Well that's not going to take you very far at all, so let me set you up with a little extra firepower here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, I was something in my glory days. Lots of suitors. Pfeh! I was a crack shot with the Rail of Adamant. You heard of it? Monstrous thing, gave it up. Gave up warring altogether! Until now, apparently!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, the passions of youth! I've smoldered away, not much of a feast for the eyes at this point. Fine. But you? Still just a kindling. Though, we'll make an inferno of you yet!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Having my Boon just stuffed into some unmarked sack, I swear! Halfway surprised it didn't burn right through. Sometimes it isn't easy getting hold of you, you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You probably could tell this was my Boon from all the heat must have been wafting from the parcel there! Surprised it didn't burn right up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, let's burn it all down, what say you, dearie?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll turn that flicker in your step into a roaring flame!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What say we make a cozy little fire, then put all our enemies in it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like we've got a properly hot night ahead of us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything's going to be covered in soot by the time we're through!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let the fire in your heart sputter out, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Hoh, let's have a look and get you good and fired up again, dearie!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, hot stuff! We're turning up the temperature to get things roaring, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Been far too long since we incinerated stuff together, hasn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes, you just have to burn everything to the ground and go from there, hot stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure everything's rotten, but between us girls, I've not felt this alive in some time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: They say never to play with fire, dearie. But you have my permission to go right ahead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Time to turn the heat up to full blast, isn't that right, dearie?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Got another little warm-up for you right here, hot stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Must be cold down in your whereabouts... but not for long!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm here again, dearie, so cozy up as we're about to have a real blast!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: This old flame can never be put out, and don't you forget it, dearie!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, if I could only roast more of our blasted enemies myself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Warmest wishes to you, dearie, and a little something extra on the side!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Some evening, mayhaps you'll settle down to a nice comfortable life, but ohh, not tonight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Sacrifices sometimes must be made, dearie. So round them up and put them to the flame!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We're in for a hot one this evening, oh I can already tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You burn proper hot even without my aid, but with it, oh, hohoho!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What's cooking there this eve, dearie? Whatever we damn well please, that's what!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well now I reckon it's time to fan the flames a bit, isn't it then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I've been cooking up a little something special just for you, hot stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Is something burning over there, dearie? Well it's about to be!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's keep that everlasting flame alive and well, what say you, hot stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well at least we're living in exciting times, I'll give us that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What say we add a little fuel to this fire we've got going, why don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: If mortals can't be having cozy-time, then I've a mind to say, nobody shall!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We could be sitting comfortable beside a fire sipping Nectar or somesuch, but no!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You're always dressed for warmer temperatures full knowing that Great-Auntie won't be far!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Time we start cooking again, hot stuff, while the night's still young and all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What say we light up the night, dearie, using whichever kindling is at hand?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I've seen an awful lot in my time, but all this lately, got to say it's a bit new!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Never let that fire in your gut go out, dearie, but don't let it burn too hot either!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let any of this get to you as it'll all blow over soon enough, always does.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Time again to let the sparks fly, eh? Oh I'm ready whenever you are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Heading to our mountain's tippy-top again? Then let's make sure you don't catch cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: If they wanted me to stay out of this, they shouldn't have gone for our home!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Sticking to the underground is not a bad idea! You're likely better off living down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought the fire goddess never got involved? In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, look at you, hot stuff! Come to help the elderly, ah what a good girl you are. And here I was just about to burn this whole blasted place to the ground myself! Now, here's a little warm-up for ya...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dear Goddess Hestia, I hope you will accept this offering. It's a pleasure to have met someone with your warmth, wisdom, and strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well look at you, making me offerings! Such hospitality, and while I'd throw most customs to the flames, the old exchange of gifts is one that I don't mind. So here, something from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please accept this, Great-Aunt Hestia! Your generous support means a lot to me. I know there's so much I can learn from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on now, I've nothing much to teach. You seem a recluse much the same as I, besides, although a looker still. But it's been nice to have made your acquaintance.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Great Hestia, goddess of hearth and flame! May your spark burn always as brightly. You've helped me so much on my way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, thank you for that, dearie, you're too kind. Mortals were so convinced I only liked burnt offerings, they never gave me good stuff such as this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A tribute for dear Lady Hestia! Your bright flames are a guiding light, and bane to all our enemies. Though your keen wisdom and fiery spirit spur me on at least as much!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: No use buttering me up, you know. What, you think I'm going to withhold the best I've got unless you pay me some nice compliment? I can be petty, sure, but not that petty!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If I'm to take another Boon of yours, Great Aunt, I bid you take another offering of Nectar from me! How you can carry yourself with such ease inspires me. If I could become more like you some ages hence, I would be very fortunate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to be like me? Now that's a first! What I wouldn't give to turn back time and have another go at things, knowing what I do now. Though come to think, we plan to turn back Time, don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grand Auntie Hestia! More and more I wonder what would it have been like if you were always in my life. Perhaps I would have grown up quicker of wit? Or happier! May we yet get to know each other like we knew each other all along!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I never wanted kids, you know, dearie. And you're not about to make me second-guess what's more than probably the smartest choice I ever made, mouthing off to me the way you did just then! Now take your blasted Boon and off with you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It must have been you who inspired me to make a warm and happy home, Great-Auntie! This Ambrosia, in turn, is for sharing with all those who make your own home feel safe and comforting, the way I feel whenever you call.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh this is right sweet of you, dearie, and you're on to something there! So many want to be famous or successful and such, but to be safe and comfortable? Nothing beats that, and I know you know. May you be always.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Even I'll admit the quaint crackle of the hearth just doesn't always do the job. Sometimes, what we need is a great big conflagration, an inferno of the highest order! How about one, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: The hearth is at the center of the mortal household, dearie, keeping my flames comfortably in place. But I can make a wildfire in a snap! And when I get angry, that's exactly what I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have had an excess of those golden coins that Chronos strewed across the land. To tempt the greediest of mortals, I suppose! Or the poorest. Not for me to judge!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: That Boatman's good at taking messages, isn't he? Almost like taking corpses down into the Underworld like he used to. Eh, nothing to do for those these days... except to burn!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You really have to pay that Charon character in these situations! What sort of daft system is that? What, no blessings for the poor? Doesn't seem fair at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: My blessing made it all that way on just a rickety old wooden ferryboat? Must be a litany of fire-safety hazards there, although plenty of water near with which to put them out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What, that ferryman having a fire sale there? Don't want you paying an exorbitant amount! Not that there ought to be a price on blessings, anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, dearie, you look terrible, what happened to you there? Well maybe you can sweat it out or something. Would that I could cauterize your wounds!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk, ooh, you've had more than a scrape or two, there, haven't you? Augh, such is life! Bite down and press on through, that's how we end up in a better spot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, looks to me like you've been through a lot! Well if you find yourself in a bad place, you just push on through! Or go scorched-earth on everything around you if you must.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh dearie, if you're all banged up from head to toe like this, then you're in no shape for the next stage of our little firefight! So pull yourself together, eh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: That there is what I call the first degree. Like letting you off with a little warning!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I like you, dearie. So I'm not going to turn you to ash this time. But later down the line, no guarantees.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So you got yourself a slap on the wrist! Might want to pour some nice cool water over that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, you're all right, and I am now all right, so I suppose that everything's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine, no need to get all hot and bothered for too long about this sort of thing. So let's just carry on!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Got just a little steamed back there, but I am back to my usual quite irritable self, thank you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, there we go again! You know they take me for the calm and peaceful one around here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Play with fire and you get yourself burned! It's just as simple as that unless I say otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, let's just cool off a bit before these flames of mine get way out of control...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I like a lass who knows how to snap back! Don't ever let anybody up here push you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine, let's turn down the temperature a bit if we're right finished squabbling for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't always quick to anger, dearie, so turns out I'm not too old to learn!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You found those little weaver-sisters did you now? Just when I thought they'd left the lot of us for good! Don't quite know what to make of their new prophecy, but time eventually will always tell with those.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: When all of this is over, I am liable to keep on wandering about I think. See how the mortals and the world are faring, can't do that as easily from on high. Olympus can be nice to visit but I never wanted to live here myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, I like a girl who's ready to gird up her loins, get her hands dirty and the like. Too many just waiting for someone to clean their messes for them, blast it all. Speaking of, why don't you go and do some blasting for me, eh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We've burned bright on Olympus I don't know how long already, dearie. Suppose our time has come and gone? Mayhaps we're more like mortals than we care to admit. Well, whatever doom awaits, I welcome it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You really are an odd one, hot stuff. At first I thought you were a little stuck up, much like some others whom I happen to know only too well. But you truly are committed to righting this wrong from before your time, aren't ya?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: What's gotten into Chronos, eh? One moment it's the same-old same-old war, and then the next, he shows up on our mountain all contrite! I know you witches can be serious but this was well past what I reckoned you could do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: I got so worked up I didn't properly congratulate you last time, dearie! Bless you for everything! You found us in the middle of a real mess and got us out. Sure we helped and all, but you did the cooking, and you did it good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Zeus and Hera had this daft old plan for stopping Typhon that involved Hephaestus and his forge and just a wee bit of my flame to get things started. This whole countryside should be glad we didn't go with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Has old Hecate not taught you to respect the elderly a little more than that? Well, here's a quick lesson for ya!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Won't do for all the rest taking old Hestia for a pushover in matters such as this. So I've got to burn you up a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't want my blessings while they're good and hot, do ya? Then this ought to warm you up in the meantime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Not really mad at you, dearie! But I don't have the energy right now to go against the daft traditions that we have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, a right good burn like that deserves another. Only fair, don't you agree, hot stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Just look at the bickering, sorry mess we've become! But we've all got to pull our weight, don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Here I was trying to lend a helping hand, but no! Somebody's always getting in my way. Well I won't have it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on now, even mortals know it's best to honor dear old Hestia first and foremost when it comes to such things!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Gave up the Boon of Hestia, did ya? Well then how about a quick reminder of the proper way to make a sacrifice!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Try as I might, I cannot help feeling a little miffed at that! And I am not someone to simply let it slide...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Now where do you get off picking one of my younger sisters over me, eh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You think you're playing it safe, but you're not. I'm through getting pushed to the side!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Why hello, dearie, getting ready for your next excursion, there? You're just a glowing ember for the moment, so how about we get you good and fired up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't go wandering into the dark without a proper light! And I don't mean the kind Apollo's offering, although mayhaps I'd settle for the Moon. Anyhow, no matter, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Think you can slip right out of there this late without my noticing? Well, typically you can, but not this time! And your punishment is a fine selection of hearth-warming Boons!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So, headed up this way again? Won't be hearing any arguments from me! Not sure you can hear me at all down there, come to think. Anyhow, let's get you all warmed up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Decided to come up for air again, hot stuff? Sure, the Underworld must have a lot to see and do, but with respect! It cannot possibly compare to everything we've got up here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You must live somewhere 'round those parts, don't you, dearie? Well, since it seems a little cold out there tonight, why don't we make some temperature adjustments here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Surviving's hard enough, but all you Underworld types, you're not supposed to be up top at all! No wonder you've gone back below, I'd do the same without a second thought!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You helped us beat that bloody monster down! Ah, if only I could have seen! You're really something else, hot stuff! Of course our good friend Typhon's already begun on his climb back up. We'll just have to make it extra tough for him...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Regards for helping clear the air a bit up here! Bought us a little moment to recover and regroup. Now everybody's all fired up again to break the siege! Augh, but listen to me! All about the warring like the rest of them...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: So let me see if I understand: Though you rid us of Typhon, you are now supposed to hunt down what remains of him, somewhere out of this bloody world? Oh you can't catch a proper break, hot stuff! But I have got your back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You know it's a bit frightening to think we're up against what could potentially be many thousands of Typhons now... Typhon possibilities I reckon is the phrase. Least we only need to deal with 'em one at a time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: They're saying that you gave him one from all of us! Chronos, that is. A right proper old fashioned beating, that is! Must have felt all warm and cozy, didn't it? Makes you want to do it again and again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: You got that bloody Titan, didn't ya? Don't feel too bad you couldn't end him altogether, not like any of the rest of us were able to, as you can plainly see. Instead, mayhaps go kick him while he's down! Happy to help with that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We've relegated Chronos to a cushy job down in the Isles of the Blessed, how nice for him, I get that. But you're still working with him to fight him to stop him from rising to power, eh? Well I suppose I get that, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We heard you tidied up one more of those bad Chronoses last night! Plenty of bad ones left apparently. The one we're stuck with might be odd, though I suppose I'm glad you didn't have to blow him up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: How many of those bad Chronoses have you wiped out already, hot stuff? Must be kind of nice seeing that smug old look on his face turn sour again and again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, hop to it, hot stuff! While you've still got your looks! Not like Aphrodite here is going to let you hold to them forever. She doesn't care for competition, you can trust me on that.

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Sweet Hestia, whatever do you mean? I'll be the first to admit looks aren't everything. Who even needs them? But on the other hand, we're blessed to have someone of such vast wisdom and experience as you here on our side!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, dearie, let's get a real fire going! No more fussing about merely with sparks and such. Oh, Apollo? Get your buttocks over here right now, 'cause I could use a light!

dialogueLines.speaker: ApolloUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Erm, right away, dear Lady Hestia! Though, might we please take more precaution not to burn down half the mountaintop this time...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: We put poor mortals through an awful lot, no thanks to Ares here. Least he's been occupied with our own troubles for a change, haven't you, laddie? That way most everybody else can catch a break.

dialogueLines.speaker: AresUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: But dearest Lady Hestia, our troubles are of grave concern to more than mortalkind! Ours is a conflict of the highest order, and irresistibly draws each of us to do our part.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Would you believe it, hot stuff? I have on good authority that somebody around here's laid a claim to being Eldest of Olympus. Oh, Demeter? Care to explain that one?

dialogueLines.speaker: DemeterUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Hestia, you had abandoned us! To be an Olympian is not merely a birthright, but a pledge; both to this family, and this mountain. Restore your rightful status, and join me now to aid my granddaughter!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Shame that all I can do for now is pepper you with Boons and such, hot stuff. I was just reminiscing here with Demeter about how we once took such matters into our own hands!

dialogueLines.speaker: DemeterUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: If your wish is to fight again firsthand, no one is stopping you, dear Hestia. Whilst we may have long since given up the weapons that gave us our first great victory, between my frost and your flames, I think we can manage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: If there's one thing I learned from my strong-armed nephew here, dearie, it's that you always have to strike while the iron's still hot. Now come on, hop to it, Hephaestus my lad!

dialogueLines.speaker: HephaestusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: I'm hoppin', I'm hoppin', Lady, augh! Though if you keep on turning up the heat in here, then this whole forge is going to blow, and take the rest of the damn mountain with it, too! And what would witchie have to fight for then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Not always big on family business, though I take exception for my little sis. She of all goddesses has to put up with the most around here, and that's not even counting all this Chronos stuff. How do you manage it, O Queen?

dialogueLines.speaker: HeraUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: I manage it one evening at a time is how, Sister! Perhaps we shall have to burn the old world down and start anew, but... I can think of none more suited, should the need arise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: Take our blessings but don't follow our example's what I say, dearie. A case in point's fish-faced Poseidon over here! Always trying hard to make a splash, never caring much who ends up getting wet. That about right, you big oaf?

dialogueLines.speaker: PoseidonUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Quite on the contrary, dear Hestia! You're wrong at least on two accounts I think: First, I need never try hard to make a splash! I effortlessly do it every time. And second, don't you call me fish-faced, you mean wrinkly old hag, ha-hah!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hestia_01
dialogueLines.text: In olden days I never took strapping young Zeus here for the marrying type, and was none too pleased when he picked my poor sister Hera for the job! But you've been a right good husband to her, haven't you, Zeus?

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: ...I sometimes pity you, Hestia, for never having known the joys of matrimony for yourself. Challenges arise in any such relationship, but together with your beloved partner, you find a way! All you must do is compromise and cooperate.
